description: Restricted contexts MUST be returned as not_found (HTTP 404) to non-audience requesters, indistinguishable from genuinely missing contexts. visibility_denied is internal-only (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.5); it is NOT in the wire-visible error enum.

scenarios.name: Producer (agent_id) requests own restricted context
scenarios.notes: Producer is always in the effective audience for their own contexts.

scenarios.name: Authorized consumer (in audience) sees the context
Unauthorized consumer sees not_found (existence-leak prevention)
scenarios.notes: MUST be 'not_found', not 'visibility_denied'. The latter is internal-only per RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.5 and is NOT in the wire enum (acdp-error.schema.json). Returning anything that distinguishes 'restricted but you cannot see it' from 'genuinely missing' would leak existence.

scenarios.name: Unauthorized consumer requests genuinely non-existent ctx_id
scenarios.notes: This response MUST be byte-indistinguishable from the previous scenario (timing differences, response-size differences, error-code differences all leak existence).

scenarios.name: Contributor (NOT in audience) requests restricted context
scenarios.notes: Contributors are NOT auto-authorized (RFC-ACDP-0002 §7, RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.5). To grant a contributor read access, the producer MUST list the DID in audience explicitly. Listing in contributors alone does NOT grant read access.
